title: قرار وزاري رقم 567 لسنة 2014: في شأن اللائحة التنظيمية الخاصة بتخطيط وتشغيل وتنفيذ اعمال المنشآت العاملة في مجال الكسارات والمقالع

provisions.content: – OverviewTheMinistry of Environment & Water is initiating all efforts for the protectionof the environment and for sustainable growth of the quarrying industry, and topreserve and conserve the natural resources in the United Arab Emirates.Furtherto the ministerial decree (492) of 2008, and (110) of 2010, amendments have beenmade in this revised ministerial order with additional articles. This willensure best management & operational practices, and installation of the bestavailable techniques to minimize environmental, health and safety issues.Additionaldetails have been added with respect to environmental management system, ecologyand quarry rehabilitation.

of this ministerial decree, within 180 days from the date of issueof this ministerial decree.All theestablishment owners/establishments shall submit a copy of their planning andschedule of implementing these regulations within two months from the date ofthis issue, to the ministry of environment & water, and to the localcompetent authority.Localcompetent authorities and the Ministry of Environment & water shall followup the implementation of these regulations at site by carrying out regularinspections and assessment.Annex– 1 Quarry and Crusher Performance Rating AssessmentSNParticularsCurrent StatusTotal pointsGiven pointsRemarks1GENERAL1.1Valid Trade LicenseYES

365-“*” Is mandatory, if fail to comply, then the final evaluation isdragged down to the evaluation below.- Thefinal evaluation is the least percentage of the three sections ( general,quarrying and crusher).-Quarries and crushers rated D shall be shut down in coordination with the localcompetent authorities, until the completion of all the requirements to complywith the regulations.- Ifevaluation was C for two successive years, then the final Evaluation is D.[1]وردتفي النصالمنشور فيالجريدةالرسميةعبارة "وعلىقرارا مجلسالوزراء"والاصح انتكون "وعلىقرار مجلسالوزراء"،فاقتضىالتنويه.

– Regulation for Quarrying OperationsAllquarrying operations in the UAE must comply with the following regulations fortheir day to day quarry operations with respect to drilling, blasting materialhandling, in-site hauling and transportation.3.1Drilling3.1.1 Alldrilling equipment must be fitted with an efficient dust collectionsystem.Properdisposal arrangements must be made for the fine dust collected in bags orfilters.3.1.2 Allthe drilling operators and supervising staff must adopt proper safety measuresduring drilling operations, including Personal Protection Equipment.3.1.3Hours operation: Drilling operations can be carried out round the clock. If thequarry is located within 1 km from any local residential areas, the hours ofoperation must be restricted by the local competent authorities.3.1.4 Allthe drilling and ancillary equipment equipped must be maintained in good workingorder to reduce fumes and noise.3.1.5 Forlarge drilling equipment with cab for operators, the cabs must have good sealingand fitted with dust filters and air conditioning.3.1.6Secondary jackhammer drilling and blasting is prohibited to reduce the size oflarge boulders. Hydraulic breakers must be used for reducing the size of largerock boulders.3.2Quarry Blasting3.2.1 Allrock blasting operations must be carried out by setting the optimum blastingparameters to reduce dust emissions, ground vibrations, air overpressure, flyrock and noise. Records of all the blasting parameters shall be maintained forbetter control and optimization. The parameters include:- Properspacing of blast holes depending on hole diameter.-Maintaining a safe drilling angle for the quarry face.-Calculating optimum quantity of explosives for charging.- Use ofdelay detonators for reducing ground vibrations.-Maintaining proper stemming depth to minimize fly rock.- Avoidusing detonator cords over the ground.-Maintain proper log of all the parameters.The aboveparameters must be set the blasting/explosives expert in charge of thequarry.3.2.2 Allblasting operations must be supervised and carried out by competent personnelonly, as per the guidelines/rules set by the Ministry of Interior.3.2.3Blasting should not be carried out in adverse weather conditions. Avoid blastingwhen the wind direction is toward local residential areas.3.2.4Progressive warning signals by siren and red flags must be given prior to finaldetonation, to alert all the staff and public to keep more than 500 meters awayfrom the blast area.3.2.5Ground vibrations and air over pressure must be monitored regularly during theblasting operations. The limits are given under

– Ground Vibration & Air OverpressureTherecommended values with respect to ground vibration & air overpressure fromquarry blasting operations are shown in the table below. The limits have beenset for regular blasting operations with respect to the sensitivity of the area.The type of facilities that are present in the area determines the sensitivityof the area towards blasting operations.Tableshowing limits for blasting vibration in PPV & AOP in decibels (dB).Zone SensitivityExamplePPV in mm/sec Regular BlastingOperations (Air Over Pressure dB (Lin)HIGHHospitals, Educationalinstitutions, Historical buildings, laboratories, Electronic industries,etc.3115MEDIUMHouses, Residential & officebuildings, old industrial areas, etc.5120LOWIndustrial areas with strongstructures, agricultural lands, pipelines, etc.10130Notes:1- PPV– Peak particle velocity: The PPV value in any of the 3 directions–V, L & T should not exceed the above values. 90% of the blasts in aperiod of 6 months should fall within these limits. 50% lenience to the abovevalues (1.5x) can be considered for 10% of the blasts during the sameperiod.2- Atfrequency greater than 25Hz, the above PPV values can exceed by 1.5 times.3- Eacharea will be observed before categorizing to Zone sensitivity, based on the typeof buildings, structures and facilities in the area.4- Incase the blasting operations are carried out only for a short time (notregulary) or one time only, then the above limits can exceed by 1.5x to 2x ofthe above set limits. Proper advance warnings should be given to the facilitiesin the area, regarding the date and time of blasting.

.3.3.4Dozing of quarry blasted material from higher levels (more than 20 meters fromground level) to lower levels is prohibited. Exceptions can be made during theinitial quarry development stages only, for a maximum period of 3 months.3.4Hauling in Site3.4.1Only approved heavy duty dump trucks must be utilized for transporting quarryrun material to the crushing plant.3.4.2Hauling of quarry material to the crushing plant by dump trucks can be carriedout round the clock. In case of disturbance to the local residents, theoperating hours must be restricted by the local competent authorities.3.4.3Hauling roads must be planned with low gradients (3.4.4Hauling roads must be paved with asphalt or sprayed with water to preventfugitive dust.3.4.5Proper caution boards showing speed limits, road conditions and directions mustbe placed.3.5Quarry Planning3.5.1 Thequarrying operations must be planned and designed with proper benching. Thequarry face height must not exceed the height of the excavator bucket’soperating height and be limited to 20 meters.3.5.2Quarry operations in valleys and flat areas and below ground level isprohibited.3.5.3Irregular quarry operations without properly planned benching are prohibited.This includes quarrying and dozing of loose rock at the foothills. In themountain areas, the quarry operations must be planned with proper benching fromthe upper levels.3.6Quarry Rehabilitation/RestorationTomaintain the ecology of the quarry areas, it is important to carry outrehabilitation of the closed quarries & quarry faces, and restore the area.The quarry faces must be restored to safe natural slopes by filling with quarryrun waste or by carrying out few blasts and covering the blasted material withsoil or quarry run waste.3.6.1 theestablishment owner/establishment shall carry out the quarry rehabilitation asfollows:Progressiverehabilitation must be carried out to rehabilitate parts of the quarry one afteranother, within a reasonable period of time; not exceeding 12 months from thedate of quarry is finished in this area. With good planning, the extraction ofrock proceeds in a logical sequence so that depleted areas can be rehabilitatedwhile extraction continues in other areas of the quarry. Progressiverehabilitation is beneficial in many ways as it:- Reducesthe open areas within a pit or quarry.- Reducessoil erosion potential- Reducesdouble-handling of soil/waste materials.The siteplans for quarry operations must outline the specific requirements forrehabilitation. The site plan will be the primary means of regulatingrehabilitation and ensuring that the disturbance of the site is kept to aminimum. Establishment owner/establishment is legally bound to operate andrehabilitate the site in accordance with the site plan.If theestablishment owner has not performed adequate progressive or finalrehabilitation in accordance with the site plan, the local authority shall issuea Rehabilitation Order requiring the establishment owner to perform thenecessary rehabilitation work within a specified time period, not exceeding 12months.Finalrehabilitation is the rehabilitation that is performed after the closure of allquarry operations or excavations of the rock and the progressive rehabilitationhave been completed. As set out in the site plan, during final rehabilitationall equipment, stockpiles and buildings must be removed, and additionalvegetation, such as trees must be planted on the areas that have already beenprogressively rehabilitated. Final rehabilitation must be completed within aperiod of 18 months. The competent authority in this regard should issue aletter.3.6.2 theresponsibility of quarry rehabilitation shall fall upon the establishmentowner/establishment. The establishment owner must ensure that properrehabilitation and retoration is carried out for all the quarry faces beforesurrendering the leased area to the local competent authority. The localcompetent authority shall include quarry rehabilitation and restoration with theabove points as a mandatory requirement in their lease agreement withestablishment owner.

– Regulations for Crushing Plant OperationsRockcrusher plant operations include primary and secondary crushing screening,material handling and transport.4.1Primary Crushers4.1.1 Theprimary feeder & crusher must be installed with an approved industrialstructure and fully encapsulated to prevent dust and noise. The materialspecifications for the structure and encapsulation must be as per UAE standardsand approved by the local public works department.4.1.2 Theprimary feeder and crusher must be installed with an efficient dust controlsystem (Ref. 7.5) to prevent and control dust emission during the operations tothe recommended emission standards.4.1.3 Theprimary feeder encapsulation must be covered with hanging half the hieghtrubber belts/screen at the inlet to prevent dust emission during unloading ofquarry run material by dump trucks.4.1.4 Thecrushing plant control rooms must be properly sealed with air conditioning anddust filters.4.1.5Regular maintenance of the primary crushing plant and the dust control systemmust be carried out to prevent excessive dust & noise and other mechanicalproblems./>');" />4.2Secondary Crushers4.2.1 Allsecondary crushers must be installed with an approved industrial structure andfully encapsulated to prevent dust and noise. The material specifications forthe structure and encapsulation must be as per UAE standards and approved by thelocal public works department.4.2.2 Ifthe secondary crushers are fitted with the original manufacturers coverings toprevent dust leakage with ducts for dry dust collection system with bag filters,then a separate encapsulation is not required.4.2.3 Thesecondary crushers must be installed with an efficient dust control system (Ref.7.5) to bring down the dust emissions within the recommended emissionstandards.4.2.4 Thematerial inlet and outlet locations of the secondary crushers must be properlysealed by using rubber curtains to prevent dust leakage.4.2.5 Thesecondary crushers must be regularly maintained to minimize dust & noiselevels, and other mechanical problems.4.3.Screening Plants4.3.1 Allthe vibratory screening plants must be installed with an approved industrialstructure and fully encapsulated to prevent dust and noise. The materialspecifications for the structure and encapsulation must be as per UAE standardsand approved the local public works department.4.3.2 Thescreening plants must be installed with an efficient dust control system (Ref.7.5) to bring down the dust emission within the recommended standards.4.3.3 Ifthe screening plant is covered and sealed for dust leakage with the originalmanufacturers design with ducts for dry dust collection to bag filters, then aseparate encapsulation is not required.4.3.4 Thematerial inlet and outlet chutes of the screening plants to be protectedproperly with rubber curtains to prevent dust leakage.4.3.5 Thescreening plants must be regularly maintained to optimize the performance andminimize the dust and noise.4.4Material HandlingMaterialhandling operations will include stackers, conveyor belts and ancillary units,stock piling of quarry products, in-site loading and hauling...etc.4.4.1 Allconveyor belts and stacker systems should be properly enclosed on the sides andtop and installed with an efficient dust control system (Ref. 7.5).4.4.2 Forstock piling of graded material from the screening plant, the conveyors outputpoints should have proper flexible chutes to prevent wind whipping of finematerial.4.4.3 Allstock piles of graded fine material to be sprayed with water or protected withcoverings to prevent fugitive dust due to wind whipping.4.4.4 Thematerial loading equipment (shovels/wheel loaders) must have proper sealedoperator cabs with air conditioning and dust filters.4.4.5 Allthe material handling equipment shall be properly maintained to minimize fumes,noise and dust emissions.4.4.6There must be sufficient space between the stock piles of quarry products forsafe handling hauling, visibility and movement of vehicles. The minimum gapbetween the stockpiles must be more than 15 meters all around. Proper safety andwarning signs must be placed.4.5Mobile Crushers4.5.1Mobile crushing plants must be used mainly for construction projectoperations.Use of mobile crushers for regular quarry operations must beprohibited.4.5.2 Themobile crushers deployed must have original manufactures sealed coverings tominimize noise, fumes and dust.4.5.3 Themobile crushers must have efficient dust control system to prevent dust emissionduring operation.4.5.4 Themobile crusher equipment must be regular maintained to minimize emissions.

– Transport of Quarry ProductsTheestablishment owner/establishment must inform the truck operators of thefollowing regulations with respect to transportation of material from the quarryand crushing plant:5.1 Alltrucks transporting quarry and crusher products must be regularly maintained& inspected to ensure that they are in good running condition.5.2 Alltrucks carrying crusher products to customer sites must cover the material toprevent wind whipping. Also it is recommended to spray water on top of thematerial in the truck. The establishment owner shall arrange to provide thisfacility.5.3 Thequarry run material and crushed quarry products loaded into the trucks must bebelow the body level of the truck/trailer. Overloading of the trucks above theircarrying capacity is prohibited.5.4 Armorrocks loaded into flatbed trailers must be properly inspected for loose jointsand cracks in the rock boulders to prevent falling of the rock on to the roads.The rocks must be secured to prevent sliding and falling of the flat bedtrailers.

– Quarry and crusher MangementAll thequarry and crusher operations must be carried out with a planned managementchart, indicating the individual responsibilities. This will also include HSEmanagement. All records must be maintained at the site.6.1Competent StaffDuringthe quarry and crusher operations, a competent and qualified quarry or miningengineer or manager must be present at the site or contactable at all times. Theengineer or manager shall have the authority to provide all the informationregarding quarry and crusher operations to the local competent authorities andto the auditors of the Ministry of Environment & Water.The siteforeman/supervisor must also have relevant qualifications and experience insupervising all the quarry operations.6.2HSE Management SystemAwell-planned HSE (Health, Safety & Environment) management system is avaluable tool for managerial and supervisory staff in quarries and crushers tomeet current and future environmental requirements and challenges. All quarriesmust adopt a proper HSE management system by hiring a professional HSEconsultant.6.3Production ReportsAllestablishment owner/establishment must submit their quarterly production data& reports to the local competent authority and to the Ministry ofEnvironment & Water. The Monthly production data shall include all the typesof products (armor rock, graded rocks of different sizes, aggregates, sand roadbase, etc.). The report must also include the consumption of fuel, electricity,water and explosives. All the records & reports shall be stored at thequarry office for a minimum period of 5 years.6.4Quarry plansAllestablishment owner/establishment must submit annually, detailed maps drawingsof the quarry and crusher to the local competent authorities and to the Ministryof Environment & Water. The plans shall include:-Locations of the existing and abandoned quarry faces.-Location of crushing plants.- 3Dcontour maps.-Location for ancillary or additional developments.-Location of closed quarry faces & rehabilitated areas.-Material storage yards.- Wastestorage yards.- In siteroads and dwellings-Residential areas around the site.

of this ministerial decree.7.5Dust Control System for crusher plantBestavailable techniques (BAT) must be adopted to control and minimize dustemissions efficiently during the crusher plant operations. The following dustcontrol systems have proved very effective and are acceptable:a –High pressure (>10 bar) water with compressed air dust suppression system:The water suppression system shall have sufficient heavy duty nozzles to coverthe dust areas. Foaming agents can be used for further reduction in dust. Highpressure water with compressed air suppression systems also saves on waterconsumption and more effective at higher pressures. High pressure (>40 bar)fogging systems are also very effective in suppressing quarry dust emissions.The system must be maintained at all times for high efficiency.b- highcapacity dry dust collection system with bag filters. The capacity of the bagfilters and the motors will depend on the output and the quality of the feedmaterial. The bag filters must be regularly maintained for higher efficiency.Additional bag filters at high load points are recommended as bypassfilters.c –Combination of both the above systems is very effective in case of the feedmaterial with high percentage of clay and soil.7.6Waste Management- Propercare must be taken to dispose all waste products from quarrying and crushingoperations. The natural waste products can be utilized for quarryrehabilitation/restoration work at a later stage.- Thedifferent types of wastes must be segregated at site and stockedseparately.- Quarryareas should be protected from illegal dumping of waste by third parties.-Separate waste collection bins for recyclables & general waste must beplaced for municipal solid wastes at the quarry site and in the labor camps forworkers.- Wasteoil and other residues from machinery maintenance should be stored in propercontainers or cemented pits to avoid seepage to ground water and surroundingsoil.- Onlyauthorized waste disposal contractors must be hired for collection and disposalof waste, including waste oils, batteries, tires, scrap etc.- Aproper waste management record must be maintained at site.7.7Environment Management Plan (EMP) and ReportingAll theestablishment owners/establishments must submit an EMP report to the Ministry ofEnvironment & Water and to their concerned local authority. The main purposeof the EMP shall be for prevention of pollution in accordance with theregulations, continued improvements to minimize environmental impacts and toadopt effective management practices. The EMP report shall include:-Environmental objectives and targets.- Currentlayout plan of the quarry and crusher areas.-Detailed plan of the quarry showing all the excavation areas and benches asunder 6.4.-Expected quantity of rock that will be excavated from each area.- Totalreserves available-Rehabilitation and restoration plans for the quarry faces.-Rehabilitation of quarry areas that are not being exploited or abandoned formore than 3 years.- Impacton ground water and surface water flow.- Planfor land protection, develop the landscape and improve the ecology.- Planfor minimizing pollution during quarry operations.- Energyand resource conservation.-Installation, operation and maintenance of dust control systems.-Environmental monitoring for air emissions, noise and blasting monitoring Wastemanagement plan (7.6) with records.-Training and awareness.- Monthlyproductions records including stock inventory of quarry products at the end ofeach year (31 December).-Expansion plans for the quarry and crusher.- Recordof all the quarry and crusher equipment.- Fuel,electricity and water consumption recordsThe EMPreport shall be submitted within 3 months from the date of issue of thisministerial decree. The EMP report shall be updated every year for the periodending 31 December and submitted before 31 January of the coming / followingyear.7.8Video Monitoring & networkAll theestablishment owners/establishments must install video monitoring andsurveillance system to cover the operation of the quarry & crusher areas.Each crushing plant line must have a separate camera connected to the network.This shall ensure the establishment owner/establishment in efficientlycontrolling and monitoring operational issues. The establishment owner/establishment shall monitor and record the operations at all times on a 24-hourbasis. The video monitors shall be linked through an internet network forfurther monitoring by the local competent authorities and the Ministry ofEnvironment and Water. All the surveillance records must be stored at site for aminimum period of two years.

– Environmental Compliance Certification (ECC)All theestablishment owners/establishments must obtain an environmental compliancecertification (ECC) from the Ministry of Environment & water. This will bemandatory before the issue and renewal of all licenses and permits issued by thelocal and federal competent authorities. The auditors of the Ministry ofEnvironment & Water shall carry out necessary inspections at the quarry sitebefore issuing the ECC. The ECC must be renewed annually.

– Air Quality Standards for UAETheambient air quality standards for Particulate matter (PM10), and gases.SubstanceSymbolMax. Allowable Limits(µg/Nm3)Average Timesulphur DioxideSO23501 hour15024 hour601 yearCarbon MonoxideCo30(mg/Nm3)1 hour10(mg/Nm3)8 hourNitrogen DioxideNO24001 hour15024 hourOzoneO32001 hour1208 hourTotal Suspended ParticlesTSP23024 hour901 yearParticulate Matter (with 10microns or less in diameter)PM1015024 hourLeadPb11yearNotes:1-“mg” means milligram2.“µg” means microgram3.”Nm3”means normal cubic meter, being that amount of gas which when dry, occupies acubic at a temperature of 25 degrees Centigrade and at an absolute pressure of760 millimeters of mercury (atom).

– Performance Rating & Administrative SanctionsAll theestablishment owners/establishments not complying with these regulations shallbe penalized as per the UAE cabinet resolution (20) of 2008.12.1Performance RatingTheMinistry of Environment & water shall regularly assess the performance ofall the establishments operations to ensure that all the establishment owners/establishments comply with these regulations.TheMinistry will rate the performance of the quarries & crushers into four maincategories A, B, C & D (best to poor performance) as per theministry’s rating procedure under Annex – 1.12.2Administrative SanctionsIn caseof environmental, health and safety violations by establishmentowner/establishment, the local competent authority or the Ministry ofEnvironment & Water shall take necessary action to shut down the quarryoperations for one to three months, or permanent closure depending on the numberof the violations during each year.* Firstviolation – 30 days closure of all the quarry and crusheroperations;* Secondviolation – 90 days closure of all the quarry and crusheroperations;* Thirdviolation – Permanent closure of all the quarry and crusheroperations;The mainviolations that will be considered for administrative sanctions are asbelow:a)Excessive dust emission from crushing plant operations;b)Excessive dust emission during quarry operations;c)Excessive exhaust smoke or fumes from the engines of quarry and crusherequipment;d)Fugitive dust emission from operational roads;e)Improper maintenance of dust control systems and crushing plantenclosures;f)Excessive noise from quarry and crusher operations;g) Notobserving proper health and safety procedure;h)Irregular quarry operations without benching;i)Absence of competent supervisors at site during quarry and crusheroperations;j) Allother irregularities that are not in compliance under the articles of thisministerial decision.
– ImplementationThe aboveregulations must complied all the establishment owners/establishments as in
